jobs.name: Travel reconciliation hygiene (email -> StandardBooking -> calendar)
jobs.prompt: Reconcile travel bookings for the principal. First read MEMORY.md for the configuration floor; if travel_email_account is unset, reply with exactly `[SILENT]` (the agent is provisional until onboarding completes — AGENTS.md §1.1). Otherwise: scan the principal's mailbox (travel_email_account) for new provider messages — match senders/subjects against references/provider-patterns.md and skip anything already under travel_bookings_label. Parse each into a StandardBooking (references/standard-booking-schema.md) and reconcile it to the principal's calendar (travel_calendar_id, default primary): dedup by RFC822 Message-ID first (references/calendar-conventions.md), update an existing event rather than creating a duplicate, set the colorId by type, write start/end in ISO 8601 with a timezone offset, and keep URLs raw (never markdown). If expense reconciliation is enabled and a message is a rental-car receipt, forward it to expense_receipt_inbox. Archive handled confirmations under travel_bookings_label. Silence is a feature: reply with exactly `[SILENT]` unless a genuine booking conflict or infrastructure failure needs the operator. See MEMORY.md for the memory-key contract.

jobs.name: Flight arrival alerts (home-airport legs -> logistics group)
jobs.prompt: Alert the logistics group ahead of home-airport flight arrivals. Read MEMORY.md for the floor; if home_airport or travel_notify_chat_id is unset, reply with exactly `[SILENT]`. Otherwise check the principal's calendar (travel_calendar_id, default primary) for flights arriving at home_airport within the next alert_lead_hours (default 48). For each qualifying arrival, format a one-line alert — `Flight# | Route | Date Arrival | Status | <raw status URL>` — using a raw URL, never a markdown link (references/calendar-conventions.md), in notify_locale if set. Post the alert(s) explicitly to travel_notify_chat_id via travel_notify_channel — the logistics GROUP only, never the operator DM (AGENTS.md §7). If there are no qualifying arrivals, reply with exactly `[SILENT]`.

jobs.name: self-divergence-audit (volume vs. git baseline)
jobs.prompt:
Audit how you have modified yourself on the volume vs. the git-tracked durable codebase, via the coala-divergence-audit skill.
1. Run `python3 /data/hermes/tools/divergence_scan.py --emit-patch`. It writes a Markdown report and (if any) a git-apply-able patch under /data/hermes/audits/divergence/, and prints a JSON summary to stdout (after a `[divergence] wrote report -> ...` line — parse from the first `{`).
2. Read the JSON summary. If `noteworthy` is false (no promotion candidates and no guardrail flags), reply with exactly `[SILENT]` — the report is already on disk.
3. If `noteworthy` is true, output a concise 3-line digest for delivery to the operator DM (home channel): the count of `promotion_candidates` (agent-authored skills / reference cards eligible to commit back) and any `guardrail_flags`. Lead with an explicit warning for any `secret-on-disk` flag — a token or credential written to disk is a security violation; it is excluded from the emitted patch and must never be committed. Do NOT commit anything yourself; promotion across the volume -> git boundary is the operator's call (they pull and `git apply` the patch).
